summary: PR #2255 decompiled `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim` in the ftCommon fighter translation unit and updated the header prototype for `ftCo_800D5600`. The PR body claims a 100% match and clean `ninja` build; the bot report records one new matched item, `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim`, worth +96 bytes.

key_files.role: Replaced the `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim` decomp placeholder with a typed C implementation.
Updated `ftCo_800D5600` from unknown return/params to `void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `UNK_RET ftCo_800D5600(UNK_PARAMS)` changed to `void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`

what_changed.text: `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim` was decompiled as a small animation-state routine taking `Fighter_GObj* gobj`.

The implementation obtains `Fighter* fp` from `gobj->user_data`, calls `ftCo_8008A7A8(gobj, fp->ft_data->x24)`, decrements `fp->mv.co.common.x0`, and calls `ftCo_800D5600(gobj)` when the counter reaches zero.
The header prototype for `ftCo_800D5600` was made concrete as `void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
The PR body reports `ninja` builds clean and `fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0`; the bot report records one new match for `main/melee/ft/ft_0D31` item `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim`, +96 bytes, from 0.00% to 100.00%.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired the recovered callsite with a header prototype update for `ftCo_800D5600`, avoiding a stale `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declaration after the call shape was known.
The implementation used existing typed fighter access (`Fighter_GObj*`, `Fighter*`, `fp->ft_data`, `fp->mv.co.common`) rather than raw pointer arithmetic in the visible diff.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a newly decompiled function proves a callee signature, update the owning header prototype in the same focused change rather than leaving `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` behind.
Small ftCommon animation callbacks can match cleanly as direct typed C when expressed as helper call, countdown update, and state transition on zero.

naming_conventions.text: Use `gobj` for `Fighter_GObj*` parameters and `fp` for the local `Fighter*` recovered from `gobj->user_data` in this fighter callback style.
The PR kept the existing ftCommon-style symbol names: `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim`, `ftCo_8008A7A8`, and `ftCo_800D5600`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function body and surrounding symbols

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: No pragmas, inline assembly, register steering, dummy locals, or padding tactics appear in the diff; the match is represented as straightforward typed C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: complete diff for changed C and header hunks

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The visible matching shape uses a subtract-by-one counter update followed by an equality-to-zero transition call.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.
Automated bot feedback reported one new match for `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim`, +96 bytes, before 0.00% and after 100.00%.

raw_diff: full diff excerpt for both changed files
notes: The loaded diff is small and complete for the two changed files, so changed-file and implementation claims are well supported.
Verification claims are reported from the PR body and bot comment; no independent build, objdiff, or regression run was performed during intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update header prototypes when a decompiled body proves a callee signature
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2255, decompiling `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim` exposed a concrete call to `ftCo_800D5600(gobj)`, and the PR updated `src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.h` from `UNK_RET ftCo_800D5600(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Straight typed C was sufficient for `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim`
accepted_candidate_records.text: The matched implementation used `Fighter_GObj* gobj`, `Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data`, typed `ft_data` and `mv.co.common` fields, a countdown decrement, and a zero check without visible assembly or steering tactics.

source_update_candidates.title: `src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.c` contains ftCommon Rebirth state callbacks
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2255 changed `src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.c` around `ftCo_Rebirth_Anim`, with nearby symbols including `ftCo_Rebirth_IASA`, and the header lists `ftCo_Rebirth_Phys`, `ftCo_Rebirth_Coll`, `fn_800D55B4`, and `ftCo_RebirthWait_*` callbacks.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as a searchable path fact, but should be curated against current source before promotion.
